# App Store submission requirements

> **Project-scoped.** Loaded on-demand by **planning** (to emit submission acceptance
> criteria into `acceptance.md` early) and **deployment** (to make the existing Fastlane /
> App Store recipe's requirements accountable — `docs/pipeline-deployment-targets.md`).
> Most rejections come from things that are cheap to get right up front and expensive to
> discover at upload. Planning's job is to turn each applicable item below into an
> **acceptance criterion** so it rides `criteria_covered` and is verified before the PR.

## Privacy — the highest-frequency, highest-cost area

- **Privacy Manifest (`PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy`)** ⚠ — required. Declares:
  - the data types the app **collects** and their use/linkage/tracking;
  - **Required-Reason APIs** the app (or an SDK) uses (e.g. `UserDefaults`,
    file-timestamp, disk-space, active-keyboard, system-boot-time APIs) with an approved
    reason code. A missing/incorrect manifest → **automated rejection at upload**.
  - Third-party SDKs must ship their own manifests; verify the ones you bundle do.
- **Privacy Nutrition Label** (App Store Connect) must **match** the manifest and actual
  behavior — a mismatch is a rejection/removal risk.
- **Permission usage-description strings** ⚠ — every accessed capability needs its
  `NS…UsageDescription` in `Info.plist` (`NSCameraUsageDescription`,
  `NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription`, `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription`,
  `NSUserTrackingUsageDescription`, Face ID `NSFaceIDUsageDescription`, …). Accessing a
  capability without its string **crashes** the app (and fails review). The string must
  clearly justify the use.
- **App Tracking Transparency** ⚠ — if the app tracks users across apps/sites, you must call
  `ATTrackingManager.requestTrackingAuthorization` and honor the result; declare tracking in
  the manifest/label.

## Accounts, auth, payments (frequent rejections)

- **Sign in with Apple** ⚠ — generally required if you offer any third-party or social login
  (Guideline 4.8). Plan it whenever social login exists.
- **Account deletion** ⚠ — apps that let users create an account **must** offer in-app
  account deletion (Guideline 5.1.1(v)). Make it an acceptance criterion when auth exists.
- **In-App Purchase** ⚠ — digital goods/subscriptions must use IAP/StoreKit, not an external
  payment processor (Guideline 3.1.1); physical goods/services use other methods. Get this
  wrong and it's a hard rejection. Flag the monetization model in planning.
- **Login not required to browse** — don't gate non-account content behind a mandatory login.

## Build, signing, capabilities

- **Code signing** via App Store Connect **API key** (no stored passwords) — already in the
  Fastlane recipe (`pipeline-deployment-targets.md`); keep the key a CI secret.
- **Capabilities/entitlements** declared and provisioned (push, App Groups, Keychain
  sharing, Sign in with Apple) match the code; mismatches fail signing.
- **Bitcode is gone; ensure a release config, valid `MARKETING_VERSION`/`CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION`**,
  and that debug logging/test hooks are compiled out of the release build.
- **Minimum device/OS** and required-device-capabilities set honestly.

## Content & metadata (checked in review)

- App Store metadata complete: description, keywords, **screenshots per required device
  size**, age rating, support/privacy-policy URLs. A **privacy policy URL** is required when
  you collect data.
- No placeholder content, no broken links, no mention of other platforms; demo credentials
  provided if login is required for review.
- Sufficient functionality/lasting value (avoid Guideline 4.2 "minimum functionality"); web
  apps merely wrapped in a WebView risk 4.2 rejection.

## Common rejection causes (design them out early)

- Missing/incorrect privacy manifest or usage strings (top automated cause). ⚠
- Crash on launch/first use — App Store review runs the app; ensure clean cold-start.
- Requesting a permission with no clear in-context reason, or before it's needed.
- Broken/placeholder UI, unreachable states, or dead buttons.
- Non-IAP payment for digital goods; missing account deletion; missing Sign in with Apple.

## Acceptance criteria planning must emit (when applicable)

Translate each applicable item into an `acceptance.md` criterion so it's verified pre-PR,
e.g.:

- `AC | PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy present, declares all collected data types + Required-Reason
  API codes | app target | build check + manual audit vs data map`
- `AC | every accessed capability has its NS…UsageDescription with a specific reason |
  Info.plist | grep capability APIs ↔ Info.plist keys`
- `AC | authenticated app offers in-app account deletion | Settings flow | UI test`
- `AC | social login also offers Sign in with Apple | auth | UI test` (if social login)
- `AC | digital purchases use StoreKit IAP | purchase flow | code + manual` (if monetized)
- `AC | release build strips debug logging/test hooks | build config | archive inspection`

Planning marks the non-applicable ones N/A with a one-line reason; it does not invent
requirements the app doesn't trigger. Deployment confirms the signing/metadata items against
the Fastlane recipe before submission.
